body_txt: Biologically based levels are pretty common, especially in Shoot 'em Up games. However, it's not always enough to explore a Green Hill Zone or enjoy some Jungle Japes — no, sometimes nothing will do but to have the whole level made out of meat. And such a videogame level that takes place inside of a giant creature or otherwise huge organic structure is referred to as a Womb Level. Expect to see lots of blood pools, deadly digestive organs and pits of stomach acids , climbable veins, deadly bone spikes or fields of sharpened teeth , pulsating hearts, and floors, ceilings, and backdrops made of pink, squishy meat. Lots and lots of living, pulsating meat. Yummy. Mysteriously, things tend to be relatively spacious, well-lit, and full of breathable air. Enemies found here usually consist of assorted viruses, bacteria, and Proportionately Ponderous Parasites — usually depicted as gribbly, visibly monstrous creatures , more rarely as Bacteria Devils — alongside the host creature's highly aggressive immune system and assorted other cells and organelles, often in the form of Anthropomorphized Anatomy . The boss at the end is often either an immense parasitic monster, a living and mobile organ — usually a brain or heart — or some form of giant fetus. This is usually because the level is set inside some kind of Enormous Eldritch Abomination , especially ones that like to swallow characters whole , or else because the characters have become shrunk down and entered the body of a regular-sized being . Sometimes it's just because the level designers felt like it. Also, because giant organs can be creepy, as can the very idea of being inside a living thing and the other sorts of organisms living inside the body . These qualities also make Womb Levels popular candidates for being The Very Definitely Final Dungeon , especially when it doubles as the game setting's Netherworld or Hell . If actual biological systems are involved, expect all laws of anatomy to be cheerfully disregarded . Especially if it is a Ribcage Stomach . Another version of this has alien bio-goo subvert and consume some previously normal technological structure. It may also be a Living Ship . When not dealing with the alien bio-goo, this is similar to a "Fantastic Voyage" Plot . The usual difference is tone and intent. A "Fantastic Voyage" Plot is typically voluntary, and involves going into somebody's body to do something to it. A Womb Level rarely provides more than a different ambiance and setting for the same sort of experience that is otherwise typical. In literature, Michael Moorcock 's Pulsating Cavern, in which Elric of Melnibone fights a quest, could well be the literal trope-namer and the genesis of many later imitators. Fortunately for most players, it's very rare for a Womb Level to end with an Anuscape Plan . Emphasis on rare, because it HAS happened. See also Belly of the Boss , for when a boss battle takes place inside of the boss itself. The polar opposite of Eternal Engine , though there may be thematic overlap if the heroes find themselves inside a giant robot. Compare Meat Moss , Bloody Bowels of Hell , Organic Technology , Living Structure Monster and Swallowed Whole . If its already dead it's a Giant Corpse World . Sister Trope to Tree Trunk Tour (botanical anatomy) and "Fantastic Voyage" Plot . For The Mood, if the setting is described in a Fantastical or Cartoonish context It could be a Sub-Trope of Wackyland , and Eldritch Location for a Uncannily Realistic Version.                                           In Midwest Angelica , the area under where the Beta Chunk lands ends up becoming one of these.
